message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "claim with more damage entries of a type than insured (e.g. two sword damages, one sword) causes non-zero exit"
Expected behavior: CLI must reject claims where the damages array has more entries of a given type than the policy actually covers items of that type (count-based check, not just presence-based).
Current error: status is 0 (CLI accepts the claim)
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts

Implement the minimal change in cli.ts validateDamages: count damages per type and policy items per type, fail if any damage type's count exceeds the policy's count for that type.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass.

content.text:
All tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added count-based validation in validateDamages. Builds a count map for damage entries and a count map for policy items, then fails if any damage type's count exceeds the policy count for that type.
- Result: All tests now pass (39 passing, 1 todo)
- Approach: Minimal — kept the existing presence check and added the smallest count comparison loop needed to satisfy the failing test.

File modified: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_16-33-29_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts
